---
title: "003 — OEM–state network evolution"
date: 2026-06-11
---

## Question

Treating the market as a bipartite OEM–state network (edge = OEM holds ≥0.5%
share in a state), how did topology — density, modularity, communities —
shift across the BS6 transition (2020), COVID, and the EV era?

## Setup

```{python}
import duckdb
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from complexity_lab.config import settings
from complexity_lab.networks import build as nb, metrics as nm

con = duckdb.connect(str(settings.db_path), read_only=True)
edges = con.execute("SELECT * FROM oem_state_edges").df()
LATEST_FULL = int(edges.year.max()) - 1
```

## Topology over time

```{python}
graphs = {y: nb.share_weighted_graph(e) for y, e in edges.groupby("year") if y <= LATEST_FULL}
evo = nm.temporal_metric_series(graphs).reset_index()
px.line(evo, x="period", y=["density", "modularity"], markers=True,
        title="Bipartite network density & modularity by year")
```

## Who holds the network together now?

```{python}
g = nb.share_weighted_graph(edges[edges.year == LATEST_FULL])
cent = nm.centrality_table(g)
cent.head(12).style.format({"strength": "{:.2f}", "betweenness": "{:.3f}", "eigenvector": "{:.3f}"})
```

## Market communities

```{python}
comm = nm.communities(g)
comm_table = comm.join(cent[["kind"]]).reset_index()
(comm_table.sort_values(["community", "kind"])
 .groupby("community")["node"].apply(lambda s: ", ".join(s)).to_frame("members"))
```

## State similarity space

```{python}
sim = nb.state_similarity_graph(edges[edges.year == LATEST_FULL], min_similarity=0.85)
sim_comm = nm.communities(sim)
sim_comm.reset_index().groupby("community")["node"].apply(lambda s: ", ".join(sorted(s))).to_frame("states")
```

## Finding

The network stayed dense through BS6 and COVID — shocks moved volumes, not
the OEM–state participation structure. Modularity is low (≈0.1): India's PV
market is one national community with Maruti/Hyundai/Tata as hubs rather than
a set of regional clusters; what regional texture exists shows up in the state
*similarity* graph, where CNG-heavy northern/western states cluster apart from
the diesel-leaning south and the EV-forward early-adopter states. Export the
GEXF artifacts (`uv run lab run oem-state-network`) for Gephi/ForceAtlas2 views.
